narrative_summary: The BRCA2 missense change p.Asp3142Gly (c.9425A>G) alters a highly conserved residue in the C‑terminal region of the protein, a gene in which pathogenic variants confer hereditary breast and ovarian cancer risk. The only data provided for this variant come from a multiplexed mouse embryonic stem cell functional assay. In that study the D3142G variant displayed a functional score profile that did not meet the thresholds for either loss‑of‑function or normal activity, and the authors therefore classified it as a variant of uncertain significance (VUS) [PMC10694496]. No clinical case reports, segregation analyses, population frequency data, or computational predictions were supplied in the extracted evidence. Consequently, the current evidence base does not support a definitive pathogenic or benign interpretation; the variant remains classified as uncertain pending additional functional, clinical, or population data.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study used a multiplexed mouse ESC functional assay to evaluate BRCA2 variants, classifying D3142G as a variant of uncertain significance based on its functional score profile.
article_mentions.title: Sequencing-based functional assays for classification of BRCA2 variants in mouse ESCs
